title: carex salina subsp. mutica
intro: Carex salina subsp. mutica, also known as salt-sedge or salt grass, is a subspecies of plant that belongs to the Carex family, characterized by its distinctive grass-like appearance and ability to thrive in coastal and saltwater environments. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Carex salina subsp. mutica include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Carex salina subsp. mutica can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to apply the medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
